title: Social Cognition Observation
description: An observation of social cognition: how people process, store, and apply information about other people and social situations. Models what is observed and keeps interpretation strictly separate. Non-clinical: never a diagnosis.

schemaVersion.description: Version of the human-mind schema family this document conforms to.

construct.description: The canonical module this observation is about. Canon: mind-intelligence-systems/models/human-mind/social-cognition.md

observation.description: What was actually observed, in plain language. Description only — no inference, no label. Example: 'Adjusted explanation mid-sentence after the listener frowned.'

indicators.description: Discrete, observable markers drawn from the social cognition construct's key processes. Each is a thing you can point at, not a conclusion.

context.description: Situational frame for the observation. Helps later interpretation without contaminating the observation itself.

confidence.description: Confidence in the OBSERVATION being accurate (not in any interpretation). 0 = unsure it happened, 1 = directly and reliably observed.

interpretation.description: OPTIONAL and strictly separate from observation. A labelled hypothesis about what the observation might mean. Never a diagnosis. Always carries its own evidence and confidence.

hypothesis.description: A tentative, falsifiable reading. Example: 'Mentalizing may be driving the real-time adjustment to the listener.'

lens.description: Which framework informs the reading, if any.

evidence.description: Pointers back to the observation(s) and any external support for the hypothesis.

alternatives.description: Other readings that fit the same observation. Keeps the interpretation honest.

confidence.description: Confidence in the INTERPRETATION. Should typically be lower than observation confidence.

provenance.description: Where this observation came from and who recorded it.

observer.description: Identifier for who/what made the observation.

reliability.description: Trust in the source itself, independent of this single observation.
